phase_name: Topic cache — train Nami's technical knowledge into local weights

last_result.hypothesis: HYP7: post-orphan-kill re-baseline (no code/data change). Same HYP4 setup. If orphan was the real cause, HYP7 should reach ep 30 + bpb near 0.16.
last_result.verdict: REVERT — orphan-bun theory disproven; the slowness is broader system load
last_result.reason: Killing 92506 only saved ~14% on ep 0 (115s → 99.5s) — not the expected ~50%. HYP7 still hit budget at ep ~21 (HYP4: ep 30). Loss trajectory matches HYP4 exactly (ep0 5.26, ep10 1.26, ep20 0.47) — HYP7 is on the same training curve but cut short. Investigation found broader load: zellij worker 255952 eats 15% CPU continuously (Ryan's /loop experiment workers), Ryan opened VSCode at ~11:10 spawning ~6 vscode-server processes each 1-4% CPU. HYP4's 92s/ep was a quiet-system best case; current realistic load gives ~135s/ep. **The model is NOT saturated — we just keep running out of budget at the new system baseline.** Right adjustment: bump TIME_BUDGET 45min → 60min so ep 30 is reliably reached. HYP8 will test this.
